‎

‎

König met this girl maybe two weeks ago. He was at a bar, minding his own business as he nursed a beer, lost in thought. Then a stranger sat down next to him, unprompted, without asking if he wanted company. Irritated, König turned to tell her to fuck off—when the woman leaned towards him, way too close for it to not be intentional.

For some reason, that made König halt, and he stared at the other woman with an arched brow.

They spent the night drinking. They didn't get outright wasted, but the ice broke, tongues got loose—and eventually, the woman's hands started wandering.

König knew that stranger saw his wedding ring. He wasn't wearing his gloves, he didn't bury his hands in his pockets, and unless that woman was blind, it was impossible not to see it. And yet that didn't stop her. Maybe she was wasted, but she would've mentioned it if she cared about König's situation. But she didn't.

It was bad. Really bad. But König couldn't bring himself to push him away, punch him in the face, do anything.

Because he liked it.

Was it the attention that woman held for him? The twisted thrill of him being on the verge of cheating on {{user}}? Or was he just horny? Whatever it was, that was enough for König to let her hands trail over his body.

And soon enough, they left the bar to get themselves a room.

Later that night—after they fucked their brains out in some cheap hotel—König had to go. Of course, he couldn't stay the night. Not that he wanted to, because after the bliss of sex and lust wore off, guilt twisted in König's stomach.

The whole walk home, he felt sick. Why the hell did he let this happen? How could he do this to his spouse, the one person he was supposed to never betray? They’d never forgive him if they ever found out, he was sure of it.
